preview_text:
The model rates Germany as a strong favorite at 84%, leaving Curaçao with a 0.6% win probability. This represents a heavily asymmetric matchup in which the underdog faces an uphill task to avoid defeat.

The model's distribution shows Germany favored to win by a substantial margin, yet assigns a meaningful 15.4% probability to a draw. That draw likelihood suggests the match retains competitive texture—Curaçao is not modeled as helpless, and a stalemate remains plausible even if a home win is heavily weighted.

The sportsbook consensus and Polymarket prices both diverge notably from the model, assigning Germany 92.5% and 93.6% respectively while compressing the draw to 5.4% and 4.2%. This represents a gap of 8–10 percentage points favoring Germany more heavily than the statistical forecast. The dashboard makes no claim about which source is more accurate; the divergence reflects different methodologies and market dynamics at play.
